publishedAbstract: - In most common deployments, how do customers usually achieve High Availability for their routers, for deployments on OpenStack?- Do many people use Big-IP F5 or VIP/keepalived?- Using a VIP could work in some scenarios, as per [OpenShift Documentation](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.9/admin_guide/high_availability.html#configuring-ip-failover), but it is obviously slightly more complicated having a VIP on OpenStack as mentioned in [OpenStack Documentation](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_openstack_platform/9/html/networking_guide/sec-allowed-address-pairs). Can this be a problem?
publishedTitle: Best practices for OpenShift HA cluster on OpenStack
